questions.question: The initial phone interview is a good time to discuss what to expect in a typical session and let the client ask questions, and to establish boundaries, structure, and a sense of safety before the first session
Sexual misconduct is not limited to sexual relations, rather it occurs when:
choices: A client propositions a practitioner, making the treatment awkward
The boundaries of either the practitioner or the client are violated
A therapeutic relationship is threatened by sexual behaviors
Practitioners trigger unwanted sexual feelings in their clients
questions.correctAnswer: A therapeutic relationship is threatened by sexual behaviors

questions.question: Which of the following is the best option to improve the energy flow in a treatment room?
choices: Place fresh flowers in each room

questions.question: HIPAA assigns a unique identifier for providers, employers, health plans, and clients
Informed consent is most critical in the context of surgery, anesthesia, and other interventions associated with direct physical risk, but is of little concern in much less consequential procedures.
When working with a survivor it is important to keep strong time boundaries during the treatment unless there are exceptional circumstances
Which of the following is NOT true about assertive language? It:
Providing suggested questions for an interview allows the interviewee to shape the program and helps ensure coverage of the important points
Which term best describes a practitioner’s character who has good boundaries, good hygiene, excellent time management skills, integrity, flexibility, and loyalty?
What are the 3 C’s of effective sales?
Which of the following attributes best describes being inspired by the joy of accomplishment?

A practitioner has begun the process for selling their business utilizing the Eight Selling Stages. The practitioner has reviewed their motives, analyzed their business, and assembled a team of advisers. Of the Eight Selling Stages, what is the next course of action?
A practitioner decides to enter into a sexual relationship with a former client. Which of the following best describes the risk to the practitioner?
Limited Liability Companies provide a shield from personal liability
Choose which of the below ethical considerations are the responsibility of the employee (select all that apply):
If there exists a power differential between two individuals in a work setting, the power differential can be temporarily reversed if the roles change

A practitioner works in an upscale day spa in a small town. Many people are related to each other and “everyone knows everyone.” One of her favorite and enjoyable clients, has been asking her questions about another spa guest. What should the practitioner do to avoid upsetting, and possibly losing, the client?
choices: Explain to the client about her ethical commitment to confidentiality and is uncomfortable with the questions
Have the spa manager deal directly with the client about questions
Recommend the client see another therapist
Tell the client she will answer the question if the client promises not to disclose the source of the answer
questions.correctAnswer: Explain to the client about her ethical commitment to confidentiality and is uncomfortable with the questions

questions.question: Which of the following reflects the Pareto principle?
choices: 25% of your activities produce 80% of your results
80% of your activities produce 20% of your results
20% of your activities produce 100% of your results
25% of your activities produce 75% of your results
questions.correctAnswer: 80% of your activities produce 20% of your results

questions.question: To save time and money when hiring an administrative staff person, a good option is to utilize a temporary employment agency
Employers are often held legally responsible for the actions of their employees
The main purpose of a petty cash fund is to pay for incidental expenses
Which of the following is a benefit of being a mentor?
When entering into a group practice, clearly written agreements with the other practitioners in the group are vital to avoid entanglements and misunderstandings
Which of the following best describes why students are at particular risk of developing dual relationships? They:
choices: Find the emotional aspects of school overwhelming
Develop close friendships with their classmates
Are less mature and don’t recognize the risks
Often develop instructional and therapeutic relationships with their instructors
questions.correctAnswer: Often develop instructional and therapeutic relationships with their instructors

questions.question: Both practitioners and clients sometimes experience sexual feelings during a treatment session
Which of the following best describes what is required for success in retailing?
choices: Choose products that are a direct extension of your work
Discuss the products’ benefits during sessions with clients
questions.correctAnswer: Choose products that are a direct extension of your work

questions.question: Which of the following is an appropriate practitioner response to a client’s strong emotional response?
choices: Use hands-on work to increase the potential for emotional release
Defuse the situation and change the focus of the session
Educate the client about the meaning and significance of the emotions
Ask questions to determine the root cause of the reaction
questions.correctAnswer: Defuse the situation and change the focus of the session

questions.question: If a client has only experienced touch in situations that are sexual the client may be more likely to interpret therapeutic touch as sexual as well
The difference between a boundary crossing and a boundary violation depends upon the circumstances.
Professionalism includes flexibility and loyalty
In healthcare malpractice actions, all healthcare practitioners are held to the same general standard of care as physicians
Personal budgeting is the best way to create a realistic financial picture
A practitioner’s office was burglarized. The computers were stolen and the office vandalized. Which of the following would have best ensured that the practitioner would have access to their records?
choices: Making hard copy files of all documents

questions.question: Which of the following best describes what contracts are particularly good for helping people navigate?
choices: The end of a working relationship
Disputes that take place in working relationships
Times when a working relationship is going really well
questions.correctAnswer: The end of a working relationship

questions.question: A company that sells items from various manufacturers is known as a distributor
Which of the following best describes how a practitioner ethically sells products?
choices: Manage product sales in the same manner as rest of the practice
Only suggest products as solutions to problems for which a client has asked help
Don’t ask a client to buy a product; let the display do the selling
Restrict product sales to items that are used in sessions
questions.correctAnswer: Manage product sales in the same manner as rest of the practice

questions.question: What component from the list below is considered necessary to be included in an operations overview?
Safety is always a consideration, but more so for someone in private practice
The memory spectrum includes which types of memories?
Generally speaking, when a practitioner works with an infant or young child, a parent or caretaker should always be present
Working with an accountant to find ways to reduce tax liability is not usually ethical
Which of the following best describe why non-fraternization policies are important?
choices: Protecting the reputation of a school
Power differential that exists between teachers and students
Transference issues that cause students to pursue teachers
Tendency of teachers to show favoritism to their best students
questions.correctAnswer: Power differential that exists between teachers and students

questions.question: It is important to establish the day-to-day activities of a practice (office logistics) along with objective consequences for when a person doesn’t fulfill their agreed obligations
What term does age, income level, occupation, gender, and education level describe?
When considering office design, the focus should be on appealing to the majority of clients while being inoffensive to them all
An individual is often considered an independent contractor when working at locations such as spas, gyms, or doctors’ offices
The best thing about unredeemed gift certificates is they add extra money for no work into your bottom line
A practitioner has recently moved into a new area. They need to bring income into their household immediately. Which of the following settings would be most conducive to their immediate needs?
Is there such a thing as equitable, mutual consent in creating a personal and social dual relationship between a healthcare practitioner and a client?
choices: Yes, if they are both adults
No, because there is a power differential
Yes, depending on the maturity of the client
No, because there is transference and countertransference
questions.correctAnswer: No, because there is a power differential

questions.question: The primary benefit of barter is:
choices: It is an efficient way to conserve cash outlays
It provides practitioners with good discounts
Getting items that would not have been purchased if they had to be paid for in currency
questions.correctAnswer: It is an efficient way to conserve cash outlays

questions.question: Which of the following most accurately characterizes clinical supervision?
choices: Provided by the educational institution the practitioner attended
A condition of employment, but not an aspect of self-employment
A place to receive appreciation for good and useful work
Required in states with licensing boards and regulatory agencies
questions.correctAnswer: A place to receive appreciation for good and useful work

questions.question: As long as a practitioner does their job well, clients will be pleased regardless of whether or not the clerical support staff is friendly and competent
Which of the following best describes the intention of an abuser towards the abused? To:
One way to broaden employment possibilities for working in a Primary Healthcare setting is by learning a variety of techniques for assorted medical conditions

What fee commission structure is common with a barter exchange?
The body of a cover letter establishes rapport and communicates your interest
Which of the following regulatory methods offers no consumer protection?
Which of the following marketing activities best describes community relations?
choices: Paying indirectly for visibility by printing business cards
Providing goodwill by sponsoring an “adopt a highway” activity
Paying directly for visibility by placing a listing in a local newspaper
Getting media coverage by being interviewed by a television station
questions.correctAnswer: Providing goodwill by sponsoring an “adopt a highway” activity

questions.question: Which part of the business plan is an outline of the organization, procedures, and policies of the business?
Which of the following is a defense mechanism in which a person unconsciously rejects his or her own unacceptable attributes, thoughts, or feelings by ascribing them to objects or other people?
Which of the following best describes prejudice?
choices: A pre-formed opinion (usually unfavorable) based on inadequate knowledge or inaccurate stereotypes
The adoption of an oversimplified opinion or image of another group of people
The belief that one's ethnicity is superior to, or significantly different from, another person's ethnicity
Treatment of a person not based on individual merit, but on the group, class, or category to which that person belongs
questions.correctAnswer: A pre-formed opinion (usually unfavorable) based on inadequate knowledge or inaccurate stereotypes

questions.question: A practitioner is joining in with two of their friends from school to create a group practice. What course of action should be taken to ensure fairness in shared items such as expenses, scheduling, and office logistics?
choices: Make a list of each person’s responsibilities and post them
Have a lawyer draw up a legally binding partnership contract
Obtain a clear, written agreement with the others in the group
Make a verbal agreement since all of the involved are friends
questions.correctAnswer: Obtain a clear, written agreement with the others in the group

questions.question: The energetic boundary involves the effects of a person’s emotional and mental state that are not expressed verbally or physically.
The client should not withdraw consent once written informed consent has been agreed upon and contractually carried out
Match the word with the definition:
prompts: A system or code of moral principles and conduct
Personal beliefs relating to what is right or wrong
Personal beliefs relating to what is good and worthy

questions.question: Which of the following items are the rarest in a service profession?
When creating a business plan, what is the component that states the highlights of the plan?
What are two types of barter?
A practitioner cannot be held personally responsible for debts and legal obligations incurred by a partnership
If a practitioner doesn’t incorporate, create a partnership, or form an LLC, the business is automatically considered a sole proprietorship
It is best to wait until you have graduated before initiating a job search
Being overwhelmed by a task often leads to procrastination
In building professional alliances, sending a mailing to targeted healthcare professionals is an example of which approach?
When creating a business proposal for a corporate wellness program at a large company, which of the following is the key piece of information to getting the contract approved?
choices: How much they will charge each employee for the sessions
The amount of time needed to complete the sessions
The type of session they will be administering
The return on investment benefits for the employer
questions.correctAnswer: The return on investment benefits for the employer

questions.question: Operational guidelines make the transition easier if a practitioner hires new employees or expands
It is preferable that other people understand you first. Once you are certain they understand you, you can work toward understanding them
It is acceptable to take deductions for pro bono work
Which of the following is the best example of a breech in confidentiality?
choices: Discussing a child’s complaint about a caregiver’s treatment with an official
Reporting to authorities when there’s suspicion that a client is neglecting an elderly parent
Agreeing to surrender a client’s files to the court in relation to a personal injury case
Telling a client’s partner about a potential health issue the client won’t deal with
questions.correctAnswer: Telling a client’s partner about a potential health issue the client won’t deal with

questions.question: Comorbidity is the presence of more than one disease and/or condition
Accidental touch is something an intentional practitioner:
choices: Plans for to keep interactions professional
Expects to happen in spite of best efforts
questions.correctAnswer: Plans for to keep interactions professional

questions.question: It is easy to find a supervisor once a practitioner has the numbers for the supervision sources for national associations
Match the terms with the best definition:
choices: A state of constant alertness to danger experienced by the survivor of trauma
Vivid memories of past traumatic events interrupting the course of life in the present
A mental health condition that’s triggered by a terrifying event
The survivor becomes numb and psychologically removed from the pain of an event

correctAnswers.Hyperarousal: A state of constant alertness to danger experienced by the survivor of trauma
correctAnswers.Re-experiencing: Vivid memories of past traumatic events interrupting the course of life in the present
correctAnswers.Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD): A mental health condition that’s triggered by a terrifying event
correctAnswers.Avoidance: The survivor becomes numb and psychologically removed from the pain of an event

questions.question: Goals that are SMARTER are Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Realistic, Timelined, Enthusiastic, and Rewarding
What is the best course of action before signing an employment agreement?
choices: Have it reviewed by a mentor
Make certain the salary is correct
Have it reviewed by a legal advisor
Make certain the benefits are stated
questions.correctAnswer: Have it reviewed by a legal advisor

questions.question: Accepting referral payments is essentially a kick-back and is unethical and in some cases illegal
To turn dreams into reality, set goals
“Ethics,” “Values,” “Morals” and “Principles” refer to the same concept.
Childhood emotional abuse may take the form of not responding consistently to the child’s emotional needs, and not being able to express appropriate emotion to the child, as well as neglect
Supervision is a nourishing, protective, and creative endeavor that benefits the practitioner and the client, but not the supervisor
Choose the FALSE statement:
choices: The power differential is increased by the therapist by leaning over the client.
The power differential is decreased by meeting the client’s body language, energy and positioning.
The power differential is increased by the client reclining on the examination/treatment table.
The power differential is eliminated as long as the power differential isn’t taken advantage of.
questions.correctAnswer: The power differential is eliminated as long as the power differential isn’t taken advantage of.

questions.question: Casual conversation with a client can easily and subtly lead to which of the following inappropriate actions?
Strategic planning is the process of breaking down a difficult problem into manageable segments
The best option to take when delivering uncomfortable information is to send an email
Blaming others for our misfortunes is part of the Pareto Principle
Which of the following is better served by having flexible boundaries?
choices: Receiving sexual advances from a client
Dealing with a chronically late client
Setting appointments with clients who have difficult schedules
questions.correctAnswer: Setting appointments with clients who have difficult schedules

questions.question: If a client repeatedly violates a boundary, even after a well-conducted confrontation meeting, it is still unethical for the practitioner to terminate the therapeutic relationship.
The key characteristic of an ethical dilemma is that something of value is compromised no matter what decision is made.
